description: Surrogate Data Collection — Healthy Run: pre-flight smoke test, disk safety check, W&B live monitoring, full 10M-transition collection, and post-collection data validation.

userStories.title: Create pre-flight smoke test script
userStories.description: As a user, I want a smoke test script that validates the data collection pipeline before committing to a long run, so I don't waste hours on a broken config.
acceptanceCriteria: Create script/smoke_test_collect.py that runs collect_data.py with 2 workers and 1000 transitions to a temp directory
After collection, load all batch files and verify: states shape (N, 124), actions shape (N, 5), no NaN/Inf values
Verify episode IDs are non-colliding across workers (unique per worker prefix)
Measure and print the actual FPS achieved during the smoke test
Print estimated wall-clock time for a full 10M-transition run based on measured FPS
Clean up temp directory after all checks pass
Exit with code 0 if all checks pass, non-zero otherwise
Script completes in under 2 minutes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Baseline FPS from this test will be passed to --baseline-fps for W&B monitoring. The collect_data.py module already exists and works — this script just wraps it with validation. Use data/tmp_smoke_test/ as temp dir.

userStories.title: Add disk space pre-check to collect_data.py
userStories.description: As a user, I want the collection to abort with a clear error if there isn't enough disk space, so I don't start a 10-hour run that will fail mid-way.
acceptanceCriteria: In collect_data.py main(), before starting collection, check free disk space on the save_dir filesystem using shutil.disk_usage
Compute required space estimate: num_transitions * 1024 bytes (1 KB per transition without forces)
Add a 50% safety margin to the estimate (multiply by 1.5)
If free space < required estimate, print clear error message showing free space vs required and sys.exit(1)
Add --skip-disk-check flag to bypass the check if user explicitly wants to
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Small change — only touches the main() function in collect_data.py. Default config targets 10M transitions ≈ 10 GB; machine has 45 GB free.

userStories.title: Add W&B live monitoring to collect_data.py
userStories.description: As a user, I want a live W&B dashboard showing collection progress and whether I'm ahead or behind schedule, so I can monitor remotely.
acceptanceCriteria: Add --baseline-fps CLI argument to collect_data.py (float, no default — user provides from smoke test)
In _multiprocess_collect, initialize a wandb run with project='surrogate-data-collection' before spawning workers
Log W&B run config: num_workers, num_transitions, baseline_fps, save_dir, use_sobol_actions, perturbation_fraction, seed
After spawning workers, run a monitoring loop in the main process that reads the shared mp.Value counter every 30 seconds

Each loop iteration logs to W&B: transitions_collected, fps_current (last interval), fps_rolling (since start), fps_baseline, schedule_delta_pct ((actual-expected)/expected*100), pct_complete, eta_hours, disk_used_gb (data/surrogate/ size), disk_free_gb
On normal completion or KeyboardInterrupt, call wandb.finish() before exiting
W&B is optional: wrap all wandb calls in try/except ImportError. If wandb not installed or WANDB_API_KEY not set, print warning and skip all W&B logging. Collection must still work without wandb.
Also log the same metrics to stdout every 30s as a single-line progress summary (works even without W&B)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The main process currently just does p.join() on all workers. Replace with a polling loop: check shared_counter every 30s, log metrics, then check if workers are still alive. When all workers finish, break. W&B credentials are in ~/.netrc (WANDB_API_KEY env var).

userStories.title: Create post-collection data validation script
userStories.description: As a user, I want to spot-check collected data for integrity and coverage, so I know it's suitable for surrogate training.
acceptanceCriteria: Create script/validate_surrogate_data.py that takes --data-dir argument (default: data/surrogate/)
Load all batch_*.pt and batch_*.parquet files from the data directory
Report summary: total transitions, total episodes, total files, total size on disk
Check for NaN/Inf in states, actions, next_states, serpenoid_times — report count per field
Verify tensor dimensions: states and next_states are (N, 124), actions are (N, 5)
Check episode ID uniqueness — report any collisions
Report per-state-group coverage stats (min/max/mean/std) for: pos_x[0:21], pos_y[21:42], vel_x[42:63], vel_y[63:84], yaw[84:104], omega_z[104:124]
Report per-action-dimension stats: min/max/mean for each of the 5 action dims
Report episode length distribution: min/max/mean/median steps per episode
Print all results as a formatted summary table to stdout
Exit code 0 if all integrity checks pass (no NaN, correct dims, unique episodes), non-zero otherwise
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use state slice constants from aprx_model_elastica/state.py (POS_X, POS_Y, VEL_X, VEL_Y, YAW, OMEGA_Z). Load .pt files with torch.load(..., weights_only=True). For .parquet, use the existing _load_parquet_batch from dataset.py.
